9.reason: [Using `gets` is unsafe and deprecated; may cause compilation error with modern compilers that remove `gets` from stdio.h]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Using `gets` is unsafe and deprecated; it was removed in C11. This may cause compilation errors with modern compilers that don't support it.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Using `gets` is unsafe and deprecated; it was removed in C11. This may cause compilation errors with modern compilers that don't support it.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [Using `gets` is unsafe and deprecated; it was removed in C11. This may cause compilation errors with modern compilers that don't support it.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [Using `gets` is unsafe and deprecated; it was removed in C11. This may cause compilation errors with modern compilers that don't support it. Use `fgets` instead.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  [Using `gets` is unsafe and deprecated; may cause compilation error with modern compilers that remove `gets` from stdio.h]

59.reason: [Incorrect indexing: should use `o[l_start + p]` instead of `o[p+t+1]`; this causes wrong number parsing after operators.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Incorrect indexing: should use `o[l_start + p]` instead of `o[p+t+1]`; this causes wrong number parsing after operators.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Incorrect indexing: should use `o[l - count + p]` or similar instead of `o[p+t+1]` because `t` hasn't changed since the start of the number, but `l` has moved forward. This leads to reading wrong digits.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [Incorrect indexing: should use `o[l_start + p]` where `l_start = t+1`, but here it uses `p+t+1` which may go out of bounds or read wrong digits.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [Incorrect indexing: should use `o[l - count + p]` or similar; current index `p+t+1` may go out of bounds or read wrong digits after operator chain.]

76.reason: [Same indexing error as above: uses `p+t+1` instead of correct offset from `l`.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Same indexing error as above: uses `p+t+1` instead of correct offset from `l`.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Same indexing error as above: uses `p+t+1` instead of correct position relative to `l`.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [Same indexing error as above: `p+t+1` is incorrect; should be `t+1+p` but only if `t+1` is the start, however loop variable `l` already advanced, so this logic is flawed.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [Same indexing error as above: `p+t+1` is incorrect when multiple operators are processed; should be based on `l` or start of number.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [Same indexing bug: `p+t+1` is not the correct offset for the digit at position `t+1 + p`. Should be `o[t+1 + p]`, but `t` may have changed in prior loops.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [Same indexing bug: `p+t+1` does not correctly point to the digits of the number after `*` or `/`.]
